method_atom: an exact inhomogeneous solution of a nonlinear governing field equation, derived under a fully general configuration with no imposed symmetries, whose free structural functions are calibrated against observational distance data; used to test whether spatial inhomogeneity in the medium can reproduce an apparent global dynamical trend (such as an accelerating effect) that a symmetric baseline model can only match by postulating an extra driving component, and quantitatively compared against that simpler homogeneous baseline via goodness-of-fit.
object_atom: the lumpy large-scale universe and its apparent cosmic acceleration inferred from Type Ia supernova standard-candle data, addressed with the Szekeres inhomogeneous general-relativistic solution of Einstein's equations as an alternative to a homogeneous LCDM/FLRW model with a dark-energy component.
brief_reason: The transferable part is fitting a symmetry-free exact solution's structural functions to data to explain an apparent effect without an added component; the cosmological expanding universe, supernovae, and dark energy are the domain-specific object.
